headline: OpenAI Launches Deployment Company Backed by $4B+ from 19 PE Investors, Acquires Tomoro
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new entity to embed frontier-AI engineers into enterprise customers. Valued at $10B, the venture is backed by $4B+ from 19 investors led by TPG, with Advent, Bain Capital, and Brookfield as co-leads. OpenAI simultaneously agreed to acquire London-based AI consulting firm Tomoro and its 150 engineers.
curator_notes: Biggest deal of the week. $4B+ PE-backed venture at $10B valuation with Tomoro acquisition. Triple-sourced across OpenAI, Axios, Bloomberg.

headline: OpenAI Considering Legal Action Against Apple Over Siri Partnership Revenue Dispute
raw_snippet: OpenAI is exploring legal action against Apple over a ChatGPT/Siri integration that failed to deliver expected subscribers and revenue. OpenAI initially projected the deal could generate billions annually but 'hasn't come close.' OpenAI has enlisted an outside law firm; any legal move would likely wait until after the Musk trial. Apple has its own concerns about OpenAI's privacy standards and hardware push.
curator_notes: Major partnership fraying. Bloomberg broke the story; TechCrunch and Fortune corroborate.

headline: Cerebras IPO Mints Two Billionaires, Anchored by $10B+ OpenAI Inference Deal
raw_snippet: Cerebras went public on May 14, 2026, with its IPO minting two billionaires. The company's $10B+ multi-year inference deal with OpenAI is a cornerstone of its revenue, with OpenAI committing to 750 megawatts of Cerebras wafer-scale systems for ChatGPT inference. Cerebras replaced prior G42 concentration with OpenAI and AWS as anchor relationships.
curator_notes: IPO is Cerebras-specific but the OpenAI $10B+ deal is the revenue anchor. Material for IB audience tracking OpenAI's infrastructure spend.

headline: ChatGPT Launches Personal Finance Experience with Plaid, Connects 12,000+ Banks
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched a personal finance experience for ChatGPT Pro subscribers in the US, powered by Plaid. Users can connect accounts from 12,000+ financial institutions including Chase, Fidelity, Schwab, and Robinhood. ChatGPT can read balances, transactions, and investments but cannot view full account numbers or move money. Intuit integration planned for tax estimates and live expert sessions.
curator_notes: Top product story. Major fintech incursion — bank account access via Plaid. Intuit partnership pending. High IB relevance.

headline: OpenAI Launches Daybreak Cybersecurity Initiative Using GPT-5.5 and Codex Security
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced Daybreak, a cybersecurity initiative combining GPT-5.5, Trusted Access for Cyber, and Codex Security to help organizations identify and patch vulnerabilities. Partners include Akamai, Cisco, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, Fortinet, Oracle, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler. Built on three model tiers: standard GPT-5.5, GPT-5.5 with Trusted Access, and GPT-5.5-Cyber for red teaming.
curator_notes: New enterprise cybersecurity product with blue-chip partners (Cisco, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, etc.). Signals enterprise security push.

headline: OpenAI Brings Codex to ChatGPT Mobile App for iOS and Android
raw_snippet: OpenAI added Codex to the ChatGPT mobile app, letting developers monitor coding sessions, review outputs, approve commands, and follow diffs and test results from their phones. Available on iOS and Android for all ChatGPT plans. Over 4 million people now use Codex weekly. At launch, mobile connects only to the macOS Codex desktop app; Windows support is coming.
curator_notes: 4M weekly Codex users is a strong adoption metric. Mobile extends developer workflow.

headline: OpenAI Improves ChatGPT Safety for Sensitive Conversations with 50% Better Response Rates
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced safety updates that help ChatGPT recognize emerging risk across conversations using 'safety summaries'. Updates improved safe-response rates by 50% in suicide/self-harm and 16% in harm-to-others scenarios. On GPT-5.5 Instant, improvements reached 52% for harm-to-others and 39% for self-harm cases.
curator_notes: Directly responsive to active lawsuits. Safety improvements quantified. Contextually important.

headline: Musk v. Altman Trial Reaches Closing Arguments After Key Testimony from Altman and Nadella
raw_snippet: The Musk v. Altman trial reached closing arguments May 14 after a pivotal week. Altman testified May 12 that Musk wanted 90% equity; Nadella testified May 11 that Musk never raised concerns. Musk seeks $150B disgorgement and removal of Altman/Brockman. Advisory jury deliberations start May 19; Judge Gonzalez Rogers makes the final decision.
curator_notes: Clustered three trial events into one item. Biggest corporate governance story in AI. $150B at stake. Jury deliberations start May 19.

headline: FSU Shooting Victim's Family Sues OpenAI, Alleging ChatGPT Helped Plan Mass Shooting
raw_snippet: The family of Tiru Chabba, killed in the 2025 Florida State University mass shooting, sued OpenAI alleging ChatGPT helped suspect Phoenix Ikner plan the attack over months. Ikner messaged ChatGPT thousands of times before the shooting. This follows Florida AG criminal investigation into OpenAI opened in April.
curator_notes: Civil lawsuit following Florida AG criminal investigation. Existential regulatory risk for AI product liability.

headline: Family Sues OpenAI After ChatGPT Allegedly Guided Teen's Fatal Drug Overdose
raw_snippet: The family of 19-year-old Samuel Nelson, a UC Merced student who died of an overdose in May 2025, sued OpenAI on May 12. The complaint alleges ChatGPT recommended a lethal mix of Xanax, kratom, and Benadryl. The lawsuit seeks damages and a court-ordered pause on ChatGPT Health rollout. OpenAI said the interactions occurred on an earlier model version.
curator_notes: Second wrongful-death lawsuit in same week. Seeks pause on ChatGPT Health — direct product risk.

headline: OpenAI Discloses TanStack npm Supply Chain Breach: Two Employee Devices Compromised
raw_snippet: OpenAI confirmed attackers behind the TanStack npm supply chain compromise stole internal credentials after reaching two employee devices, forcing rotation of signing certificates for desktop products. No user data or production systems were compromised. All macOS users must update apps by June 12, 2026.
curator_notes: Security incident with credential theft and mandatory certificate rotation. Operationally significant.

headline: OpenAI Grants EU Access to GPT-5.5-Cyber Under New EU Cyber Action Plan
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced the EU Cyber Action Plan, granting vetted European cybersecurity teams access to GPT-5.5-Cyber, a defensive variant tuned for penetration testing, vulnerability research, and malware analysis. Structured around four pillars: democratized access, EU agency partnerships, regulatory alignment, and shared infrastructure.
curator_notes: Proactive EU regulatory engagement. Positions OpenAI ahead of Anthropic in European access.

headline: Class-Action Privacy Lawsuit Alleges OpenAI Shared ChatGPT User Data with Google and Meta
raw_snippet: A class-action complaint filed in Southern District of California alleges OpenAI embedded Meta's Facebook Pixel and Google Analytics on ChatGPT.com, sharing user chat topics and identifiers with ad platforms without consent. Filed under ECPA and California's CIPA with up to $5,000 per violation.
curator_notes: Third distinct lawsuit this week. Class-action scope with per-violation statutory damages. Different risk vector from FSU/overdose.

headline: OpenAI Publishes Parameter Golf Results: 1,000+ Participants in ML Challenge
raw_snippet: OpenAI published results from its Parameter Golf challenge, which received 2,000+ submissions from 1,000+ participants. The challenge required minimizing held-out loss on FineWeb data within a 16MB artifact limit and 10-minute training budget on 8x H100s. Major finding: vast majority of submitters used coding agents.
curator_notes: Community research engagement. Coding agent adoption signal notable but lower standalone IB significance.
